seoTitle: How to Use the Zendesk Ticket System (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn the core Zendesk ticket workflow: filtering views, replying to customers, leaving internal notes, using macros, and closing out resolved tickets.
intro: Getting comfortable with the Zendesk ticket workflow is the fastest way to stop feeling overwhelmed by an incoming support queue. This guide covers navigating dashboard views, distinguishing internal notes from public replies, using macros to save time, and properly closing out tickets once an issue is resolved.

steps.heading: Navigate your dashboard views
steps.body: Use the main dashboard to filter and track open, pending, and unassigned ticket queues.

steps.heading: Review the ticket history
steps.body: Open an active ticket and review the customer's profile and past interactions before responding.

steps.heading: Use internal notes for collaboration
steps.body: Switch to internal notes mode to loop in teammates privately without the customer seeing the comment.

steps.heading: Apply canned responses
steps.body: Switch back to public reply mode and use macros to apply pre-written responses to common questions instantly.

steps.heading: Update ticket metadata
steps.body: Adjust fields like Type, Priority, and Assignee to keep the ticket properly categorized and routed.

steps.heading: Close out resolved tickets
steps.body: Once the issue is handled, submit the ticket as solved to close it and update your reporting.

faq.question: What's the difference between an internal note and a public reply?
faq.answer: Internal notes are only visible to your team, while public replies are sent directly to the customer.

faq.question: Can I undo marking a ticket as solved?
faq.answer: Yes, you can typically reopen a solved ticket by changing its status, though your workflow may set a window before it fully closes.

faq.question: How do macros save time on repeat questions?
faq.answer: Macros let you apply a saved combination of text, tags, and status changes with a single click instead of typing everything manually.

faq.question: What happens if a ticket isn't assigned to anyone?
faq.answer: Unassigned tickets typically sit in a shared queue until an agent claims them, which is why checking that view regularly matters.
